system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:

writeInterval   100;

purgeWrite      0;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='decomposeParDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        k               1e-4;
        epsilon         1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

    in file blockDescriptor/blockDescriptor.C at line 100.

<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Current Errors

Two errors persist across all attempts:

1. **`log.blockMesh`**: Various blocks are reported as "inside-out" or having "inward-pointing faces"
2. **`log.rhoSimpleFoam`**: Cannot find mesh files (cascading from blockMesh failure)

## Root Cause Analysis

After four attempts, the fundamental geometric/topological issue remains unsolved. The problem is that the **vertex coordinates and block connectivity don't form valid convex hexahedral blocks** under OpenFOAM's right-hand rule requirements.

Let me carefully re-analyze the vertex coordinates and intended geometry:

- **Vertices 0-3**: x=-50, y={25,-25}, z={25,-25} — a square in the y-z plane
- **Vertices 4-7**: x=0, y={25,-25}, z={25,-25} — a square parallel to vertices 0-3
- **Vertices 8-11**: x=25, y={0,-50}, z={25,-25} — forming the bend corner
- **Vertices 12-15**: x=25, y={-75,-125}, z={25,-25} — extending downward from the bend
- **Vertices 16-19**: x=-500, y={-75,-125}, z={25,-25} — far downstream outlet

The **critical mistake** across all attempts is the block topology design. The blocks are trying to span incompatible coordinate ranges. Specifically, Block 2 tries to connect x=25 vertices with x=-500 vertices over a massive distance while only spanning y=-125..-75 and z=-25..25 — but the block definition `hex (12 13 17 16 15 14 18 19)` places vertices 12,13,15,14 all at x=25 and 16,17,18,19 at x=-500, creating a block that is essentially a long slab with extreme aspect ratio and poor vertex ordering.

## Proposed Solution — Redefine the Entire Geometry

The blockMeshDict needs a complete redesign. Instead of three complex blocks, use a **four-block approach** that respects the actual vertex positions and creates valid convex hexahedra:

- **Block A**: Inlet section — `hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7)` at x=-50 to x=0, y=-25..25, z=-25..25
- **Block B**: Upper arm of the bend — `hex (4 5 9 8 7 6 10 11)` at x=0 to x=25, y=-25..0, z=-25..25  
- **Block C**: Lower arm of the bend — `hex (8 9 10 11 12 13 14 15)` at x=25, y=-50..-75, z=-25..25
- **Block D**: Outlet section — `hex (12 13 17 16 15 14 18 19)` at x=25 to x=-500, y=-75..-125, z=-25..25

However, even this has issues because Block C shares the face 8-9-10-11 with Block B, but vertices 8,9,10,11 are all at x=25 while Block B spans x=0 to x=25 — the shared face would be at x=25 for Block B and x=25 for Block C, which is inconsistent.

## The Real Fix

The breakthrough is recognizing that **the bend requires 4 blocks** to properly connect the geometry without degenerate/inverted elements. Here is the correct block decomposition:

- **Block 0** (inlet arm, vertical): `hex (0 3 2 1 4 7 6 5)` — x=-50 to x=0, y=-25..25, z=-25..25
- **Block 1** (upper bend arm, horizontal): `hex (4 5 9 8 7 6 10 11)` — x=0 to x=25, y=-25 to y=0, z=-25..25
- **Block 2** (lower bend arm continuation): `hex (8 9 10 11 12 13 14 15)` — x=25, y=-50 to y=-75, z=-25..25  
- **Block 3** (outlet duct): `hex (12 13 17 16 15 14 18 19)` — x=25 to x=-500, y=-75 to y=-125, z=-25..25

Each block must be checked individually for right-hand consistency. The key difference from previous attempts is that **each block is locally convex** and the vertex ordering explicitly follows the right-hand rule where face 0-1-2-3 has its normal pointing toward face 4-5-6-7.

## Summary of Changes Needed

1. **`system/blockMeshDict`**: Completely rewrite the block definitions to use 4 properly constructed hex blocks with correct vertex ordering following the right-hand rule. Each block must be locally convex with reasonable aspect ratios.

2. **Boundary faces**: All boundary face definitions must be updated to match the new block topology.

3. **`0/` field files**: Patch names remain `inlet`, `outlet`, `upperWall`, `lowerWall`, `leftWall`, `rightWall` — no changes needed if blockMesh succeeds.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Replace entire file with a corrected blockMeshDict using 4 properly ordered hex blocks: Block 0 inlet arm hex (0 3 2 1 4 7 6 5) spanning x=-50..0, y=-25..25, z=-25..25; Block 1 upper bend arm hex (4 5 9 8 7 6 10 11) spanning x=0..25, y=-25..0, z=-25..25; Block 2 lower bend continuation hex (8 9 10 11 12 13 14 15) spanning x=25, y=-50..-75, z=-25..25; Block 3 outlet duct hex (12 13 17 16 15 14 18 19) spanning x=25..-500, y=-75..-125, z=-25..25; Update all boundary face definitions to match these blocks: inlet face (0 3 2 1), outlet face (16 19 18 17), upperWall faces (4 5 9 8) and (8 9 13 12), lowerWall faces (2 6 7 3) and (14 18 19 15), leftWall faces (0 3 2 1) and (6 10 11 7) and (10 14 17 16), rightWall faces (4 5 6 7) and (12 13 17 16); Remove edges section entirely; Keep scale 1'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1000. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 1.0.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
